description: Implement parallel worktree-isolated sched drain per ADR-2604151430. P1 introduces the slot table + max_parallel_drains config. P2 adds the worktree create/dispatch path (each running task gets feat/sched-drain/<task-id>/). P3 adds the static file-overlap conflict gate. P4 adds tier-aware throttles (T2.5, T3 caps). P5 adds gc-worktrees + post-merge cleanup. P6 covers tests, build, and docs.

tasks.title: Replace single `current_task: Option<Task>` daemon state with `slots: Vec<DrainSlot>` where DrainSlot { slot_id, task_id, worktree_path, started_at, child_pid }. Slot count = max_parallel_drains (default 3, persisted in .hex/project.json under sched.max_parallel_drains, env override HEX_SCHED_MAX_PARALLEL).
queue_drain becomes drain_step: examines free slots, walks pending tasks in FIFO order, promotes the first task that passes the conflict + tier gates (gates land in P3/P4) into the first free slot.
Unit tests: slot allocation FIFO, slot release on task completion, max_parallel_drains respected when 5 tasks queued and cap=3, env var overrides .hex/project.json.

On promotion, sched daemon creates a worktree at feat/sched-drain/<task-id>/ on a fresh branch sched/<task-id> by shelling out to the existing `hex worktree create`. Sets the spawned subprocess CWD to the worktree path. Stores worktree_path in DrainSlot.

On task success (evidence-guard passes per ADR-2604141400), sched daemon shells out to `hex worktree merge feat/sched-drain/<task-id>` to integrate. On merge success, run `hex worktree cleanup feat/sched-drain/<task-id>`. On merge failure, leave worktree, mark task failed with reason 'merge_failed: <stderr>'.
Pre-flight: before promoting any task, daemon runs `git status --porcelain` on main; if dirty, log warning ('main has uncommitted changes — pausing parallel drain until clean') and skip this tick.

Integration test: enqueue 2 disjoint workplans into a tmpdir git repo, drive 4 daemon ticks, assert both worktrees were created, both subprocesses ran, both merged back to main, both worktrees were cleaned up.

phases.title: Worktree create + dispatch + merge per slot

tasks.title: Add fn workplan_file_set(path: &Path) -> HashSet<PathBuf> that loads a workplan JSON and returns the union of phases[].tasks[].files[]. Empty/missing files arrays return empty set (treated as 'unknown — block parallel by default').

drain_step gate: a candidate workplan task is only promoted if its file_set is non-empty AND disjoint from every running slot's file_set. If file_set is empty (workplan didn't declare files), do NOT promote concurrently — wait until all other slots are free (conservative-by-default).

Test: workplan A claims [src/a.rs], workplan B claims [src/b.rs] → both promote concurrently. Workplan C claims [src/a.rs] while A is running → C waits. Workplan D with empty files[] → waits until all slots empty.

Add fn workplan_max_tier(path: &Path) -> Tier — returns max(strategy_hint→tier) across all tasks. strategy_hint mapping: scaffold/transform/script→T1, codegen→T2, inference→T2.5; absent hint defaults to T2.

Per-tier slot caps in .hex/project.json: sched.max_parallel_drains_t25 (default 1), sched.max_parallel_drains_t3 (default 1). drain_step counts running slots by tier; if promoting candidate would exceed its tier's cap, skip.
Test: with t25 cap=1, two T2.5 workplans queued → only one promotes. Once first completes, second promotes. Mixed T1+T2.5 with disjoint files → both promote (different tier buckets).

Add `hex sched gc-worktrees` subcommand: lists all feat/sched-drain/<task-id>/ worktrees; for each, look up the task in the queue; if task status is failed AND task age > N days (default 7, --days flag) AND user passes --force, run `hex worktree cleanup` on it. Without --force, lists what would be cleaned (dry-run).
On daemon startup, log any orphan feat/sched-drain/ worktrees (worktree exists but task is gone from queue) with a one-line warning + suggestion to run `hex sched gc-worktrees`.
Workspace build gate: cargo check --workspace + cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast.

Rebuild release binaries: cargo build -p hex-cli --release. Smoke: enqueue 2 disjoint dummy workplans, observe both promote concurrently in `hex sched queue list` (depends on ADR-2604151400 having shipped first — list this dependency in the workplan description).
Update CLAUDE.md sched section: document max_parallel_drains, file-overlap gate, tier caps, gc-worktrees command. Cross-reference ADR-2604151430.
